rulebook:
# CLAUDE.md — AI Agent Rulebook
# This file governs ALL code generated by the agent. Never deviate from these rules.

## Architecture Rules
- All service classes MUST be `sealed`
- All public methods MUST be `async Task` or `async Task<T>`
- Constructor injection ONLY — no service locator, no static calls
- Repository pattern for all data access (no raw SQL strings)
- 3-tier architecture: Controller → Service → Repository

## C# Code Standards
- PascalCase for classes, methods, properties
- camelCase for local variables and parameters
- Suffix: Services end in `Service`, Controllers end in `Controller`
- Method names: Verb + Noun + Async (e.g., `ProcessOrderAsync`)
- XML doc comments on ALL public methods

## Forbidden Patterns (auto-blocked by validator)
- `dynamic` keyword — NEVER use
- `object` type for typed data — NEVER use
- Bare catch blocks: `catch { }` or `catch (Exception) { }` with no logging
- SQL string concatenation: `"SELECT * FROM " + variable`
- `Session["key"]` direct access — use ISessionService instead
- `Response.Write(...)` — use Razor views only
- Synchronous DB calls — always await

## Migration Targets
- VBScript Sub/Function → sealed C# Service class (one method per Sub)
- Classic ASP SQL block → EF Core DbContext with typed entities
- Classic ASP UI block → Controller Action + ViewModel + Razor View
- COM objects → Registered .NET services via DI container
- Request.Form["x"] → Strongly-typed ViewModel properties
migration_patterns:
# migration-patterns.md — Legacy → Modern Translation Dictionary ## VBScript → C# Patterns | Legacy (VBScript) | Modern (.NET Core) | |-------------------------------|-------------------------------------------| | `Dim x` | `var x` or explicit typed declaration | | `Sub ProcessOrder()` | `public async Task ProcessOrderAsync()` | | `Function GetTotal()` | `public async Task GetTotalAsync()` | | `Set conn = CreateObject(...)` | Constructor-injected `AppDbContext` | | `conn.Execute "UPDATE ..."` | `await _context.SaveChangesAsync()` | | `rs.Fields("Name")` | `entity.Name` (typed EF Core entity) | | `On Error Resume Next` | `try/catch` with structured logging | | `MsgBox "Error"` | `_logger.LogError(ex, "message")` | | `Err.Number <> 0` | Typed exception handling | | `WScript.Echo` | Return typed result from method | ## Classic ASP → Razor MVC Patterns | Legacy (Classic ASP) | Modern (Razor MVC) | |----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------| | `Request.Form("OrderId")` | `[FromForm] OrderViewModel model` | | `Response.Write("

" & name & "

")` | `

@Model.Name

` in .cshtml | | `Session("UserId")` | `ISessionService.GetCurrentUserId()` | | `"SELECT * FROM Orders WHERE Id=" & id` | `_context.Orders.FindAsync(id)` | | `rs.Open sql, conn` | `await _context.Orders.ToListAsync()` | | `rs("Name")` | `order.Name` (typed EF Core entity) | | `<% ... %>` (code blocks) | Controller Action method | | `<% Response.Write(x) %>` | `@Model.X` in Razor view | | `rs.EOF` | `.FirstOrDefaultAsync()` returning null check| ## COM Object Registry | COM Object | .NET Core Replacement | |------------------------------|-------------------------------------------| | `ADODB.Connection` | `AppDbContext` (EF Core) | | `ADODB.Recordset` | `List` or `IQueryable` | | `MSXML2.DOMDocument` | `System.Xml.Linq.XDocument` | | `Scripting.FileSystemObject` | `System.IO.File` / `System.IO.Directory` | | `CDO.Message` (email) | `IEmailService` with MailKit | | `WScript.Shell` | `System.Diagnostics.Process` |

target_unit.description: Response.Write calls and HTML markup extracted from order_detail.asp
warnings: Business logic detected in view layer — will be moved to Controller/Service during migration

agent_instructions:
SLASH COMMAND: /modernize-asp-view
TASK: Migrate the Classic ASP UI layer to Razor MVC (3 output files).

RULES (from CLAUDE.md — non-negotiable):
1. NO Response.Write — all output via Razor @Model syntax
2. NO Session[] direct access — use ISessionService
3. Business logic detected in view — move to Controller/Service
4. ViewModel must be strongly typed

OUTPUT STRUCTURE (3 files from 1 .asp file):
  File 1: output/Controllers/OrderController.cs  (async action methods)
  File 2: output/ViewModels/OrderDetailViewModel.cs  (typed properties)
  File 3: output/Views/Order/Detail.cshtml  (Razor view, @model OrderDetailViewModel)

WARNINGS TO ADDRESS: ['Business logic detected in view layer — will be moved to Controller/Service during migration']
